    "evidence": "文件: fastapi/dependencies/utils.py\n@@ -774,8 +777,8 @@ def _get_multidict_value(\n             and len(value) == 0\n         )\n     ):\n-        if field.field_info.is_required():\n-            return\n+        if form_input or field.field_info.is_required():\n+            return None",
    "advice": "建议当 `form_input=True` 且字段为必填时，保持原有的行为，即不返回任何值（隐式返回 `None` 或使用 `return` 语句），而不是显式返回 `None`。这样可以确保字段在传递给 Pydantic 的数据字典中完全缺失，与 JSON 端点的行为保持一致。",
    "confidence": 0.8,
    "severity": "Medium"
  },
  {
    "type": "Readability",
    "description": "在 `_get_multidict_value` 函数中，条件 `(isinstance(field.field_info, params.Form) or form_input)` 的逻辑可能不够清晰。当 `form_input=True` 时，无论 `field.field_info` 是否为 `params.Form` 类型，该条件都为真。这可能导致读者困惑，因为 `form_input` 参数名暗示它仅用于表单输入，但实际逻辑却将其与 `params.Form` 类型检查合并。",
    "evidence": "文件: fastapi/dependencies/utils.py\n@@ -765,7 +768,7 @@ def _get_multidict_value(\n     if (\n         value is None\n         or (\n-            isinstance(field.field_info, params.Form)\n+            (isinstance(field.field_info, params.Form) or form_input)\n             and isinstance(value, str)  # For type checks\n             and value == \"\"\n         )",
    "advice": "建议将条件拆分为两个独立的部分，以提高可读性。例如，可以分别处理 `form_input=True` 的情况和 `field.field_info` 为 `params.Form` 的情况，或者添加注释说明 `form_input` 参数的作用和逻辑。",

commits:
[{'sha': 'f818eac7d79ca6843d2cb7885eed1b52d5d41c34', 'message': 'Fix form model pre-filling defaults, breaking model_fields_set

When a Pydantic BaseModel is used as a Form() parameter, fields that
were absent from the submission or submitted as blank strings ("") were
being pre-filled with their default values before model construction.
Pydantic then treated those pre-filled defaults as explicitly-set values,
making model_fields_set incorrect and preventing model validators from
distinguishing unset fields from fields set to their default.

Two cases needed fixing:

1. Absent fields - the field key is not in the form data at all
   (e.g. an unchecked HTML checkbox, which browsers omit entirely).

2. Blank string fields - the field key is present but with value ""
   (e.g. a cleared text input, which browsers send as key=).

Fix: add form_input=True parameter to _get_multidict_value. When set,
both absent and blank-string values return None rather than the field
default. _extract_form_body now calls _get_multidict_value(form_input=True)
so neither case gets pre-populated into the values dict. Pydantic then
applies defaults through its own mechanism, which correctly leaves those
fields out of model_fields_set.

Required fields are unaffected: Pydantic still raises a validation error
when a required field is absent from the submitted data.

Closes #13399

Co-authored-by: sneakers-the-rat <sneakers-the-rat@protonmail.com>'}, {'sha': '6edfbcaf7d36d647bd777859293417bb6b0832cf', 'message': '🎨 Auto format'}]

review:
[
  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "在 `_get_multidict_value` 函数中，当 `form_input=True` 且字段为必填时，函数返回 `None`。然而，对于必填字段，当值缺失时，正确的行为应该是返回 `None` 以触发 Pydantic 的验证错误，而不是返回字段的默认值。此变更确保了这一点，但需要检查是否与函数其他部分的逻辑一致。证据显示，当 `form_input` 为 `True` 或字段为必填时，函数返回 `None`。这修复了之前的问题，即必填字段在缺失时可能错误地返回默认值，导致验证错误信息中的 `input` 字段不正确（如测试所示）。",
    "evidence": "在 `fastapi/dependencies/utils.py` 的变更中，第 777-778 行：`if form_input or field.field_info.is_required(): return None`。这改变了之前仅当 `field.field_info.is_required()` 为真时才返回 `None` 的逻辑。在 `tests/test_forms_single_model.py` 的变更中，错误响应的 `input` 字段从 `{\"tags\": [\"foo\", \"bar\"], \"with\": \"nothing\"}` 变为 `{}`，表明修复后，缺失的必填字段不再错误地包含其他字段的默认值。",
    "advice": "此变更符合项目修复表单默认值预填充导致 `model_fields_set` 错误的目标。建议保持此逻辑，因为它确保了必填字段缺失时触发正确的验证错误，并且与 Pydantic 的行为一致。",
    "confidence": 0.95,
    "severity": "Medium"
  },
  {
    "type": "Readability",
    "description": "在 `_get_multidict_value` 函数中，新增的 `form_input` 参数默认值为 `False`，但在 `_extract_form_body` 中调用时显式设置为 `True`。这可能导致混淆，因为参数名 `form_input` 暗示与表单输入相关，但函数也处理其他类型的参数（如查询参数）。虽然变更的目的是修复表单特定问题，但参数命名可能不够清晰，未能明确其仅用于处理表单中缺失或空字符串值的情况。",
    "evidence": "在 `fastapi/dependencies/utils.py` 的变更中，第 754 行新增参数 `form_input: bool = False`，并在第 919 行调用 `_get_multidict_value(field, received_body, form_input=True)`。函数注释或文档字符串未更新以解释此参数的目的。",
    "advice": "建议更新函数 `_get_multidict_value` 的文档字符串，明确说明 `form_input` 参数的作用：当设置为 `True` 时，对于表单输入，缺失或空字符串值将返回 `None` 而不是默认值，以确保 `model_fields_set` 的正确性。这符合项目对可读性和维护性的要求。",
    "confidence": 0.8,
    "severity": "Low"
  },
  {
    "type": "Design",
    "description": "变更在 `_get_multidict_value` 函数中引入了对 `form_input` 参数的检查，以决定是否将空字符串视为未设置值。然而，逻辑 `(isinstance(field.field_info, params.Form) or form_input)` 可能导致重复检查，因为当 `form_input` 为 `True` 时，`field.field_info` 可能已经是 `params.Form` 实例。这虽然不影响功能，但可能使条件判断略显冗余。",
    "evidence": "在 `fastapi/dependencies/utils.py` 的变更中，第 768 行：`(isinstance(field.field_info, params.Form) or form_input)`。当 `_extract_form_body` 调用时设置 `form_input=True`，且 `field.field_info` 是 `params.Form` 实例，条件为真。这确保了空字符串被正确处理，但逻辑可以简化。",
    "advice": "考虑到变更的明确目的是修复表单问题，且 `form_input` 参数仅在表单提取中使用，建议保持当前逻辑以确保清晰性。或者，可以添加注释说明 `form_input` 参数用于覆盖 `field.field_info` 检查，以处理表单特定场景。这符合项目设计一致性的目标。",
    "confidence": 0.7,
    "severity": "Low"
  }
]

  {
    "type": "Readability",

    "evidence": "文件: fastapi/dependencies/utils.py\n@@ -765,7 +768,7 @@ def _get_multidict_value(\n     if (\n         value is None\n         or (\n-            isinstance(field.field_info, params.Form)\n+            (isinstance(field.field_info, params.Form) or form_input)\n             and isinstance(value, str)  # For type checks\n             and value == \"\"\n         )",

    "evidence": "文件: fastapi/dependencies/utils.py\n@@ -916,7 +919,7 @@ async def _extract_form_body(\n     values = {}\n \n     for field in body_fields:\n-        value = _get_multidict_value(field, received_body)\n+        value = _get_multidict_value(field, received_body, form_input=True)",

[{'sha': '0d5ecfae6747bcf2b13ffe1a440eaf860b7930b6', 'message': 'Fix mounting sub-applications under APIRouter (#10180)

Sub-apps mounted on an APIRouter were silently dropped when the router
was included into a FastAPI app via include_router(), causing 404s for
any path under the mount (e.g. GET /api/subapi/sub).

Two changes:

1. APIRouter.mount() override — prepends self.prefix to the path before
   delegating to Router.mount(), matching the behaviour of add_api_route
   which already does `self.prefix + path`. This ensures the stored
   route.path is consistent whether the mount is registered on the router
   or on the app directly.

2. include_router() Mount branch — a new `elif isinstance(route, routing.Mount)`
   case forwards mounted sub-applications with `prefix + route.path`,
   exactly as is done for every other route type (Route, APIRoute,
   WebSocketRoute).

Both prefixes compose correctly: router-level prefix and include_router
prefix stack in the same way as for ordinary endpoints.

Fixes #10180'}]

[{'sha': 'f6f4c7d0dae134e5d1cf147ba64f9222d7a789ae', 'message': "fix: include method in operation_id to prevent duplicates with multi-method routes

When a route is defined with multiple HTTP methods (e.g., methods=['POST', 'DELETE']),
the operation_id generation was only using the first method name, causing all
methods to get the same operation_id suffix. This resulted in duplicate operation IDs
in the OpenAPI schema and incorrect warnings.

Changes:
- Modified generate_unique_id() to accept an optional 'method' parameter
- When a specific method is provided, it includes that method in the operation_id
- Updated get_openapi_operation_metadata() in openapi/utils.py to pass the method
- Each HTTP method now gets its own unique operation_id

Fixes #13175"}, {'sha': '97acb87acfb8e183e74874e8aec4f21f109bd776', 'message': 'test: add test for duplicate operation ID fix with multi-method routes'}, {'sha': 'a93a92f187a8c985e71870f06624c4d2c01e12c5', 'message': '🎨 Auto format'}]

commits:
[{'sha': '158d52945a256d66369a98a0d667121905290bf5', 'message': 'Fix form model losing model_fields_set for unsubmitted fields

When a Pydantic BaseModel is used as a Form parameter, fields absent from
the form submission were being pre-filled with their default values before
the model was constructed. This caused Pydantic to treat those defaults as
explicitly-set values, so model_fields_set incorrectly included fields the
client never sent.

The fix skips absent fields in _extract_form_body entirely, letting Pydantic
apply defaults through its own mechanism (which correctly leaves them out of
model_fields_set), matching the behaviour of JSON body endpoints.

Fixes #13399'}]

Please, review existing PRs linked to the issue (including closed PRs, to understand the reasons they were closed) before opening your PRs. And provide an explanation why you think your approach is better. See https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/pull/13944', '@YuriiMotov thanks for the pointer. I reviewed all related PRs: - #13944 (yours, open): supports StaticFiles only, raises `FastAPIError` for other ASGI apps - #14032 (sujin502, closed): generic Mount support, closed for inactivity - #14924, #14926 (kimchikingdom, closed): generic Mount, you redirected to #13944 - #14793 (jonathan-fulton, open): generic Mount support - #14984 (fcarvajalbrown, closed), #15014 (Anandesh-Sharma, closed): same approach as ours I understand your position: sub-apps should be mounted to the root app, and only StaticFiles makes sense on a sub-router. Looking at the issue comments, there are real use cases beyond StaticFiles that users are requesting: - Mounting v1/v2 API versions under a shared prefix router - Serving Dash dashboards alongside API routes on the same router - Adding authentication dependencies to third-party ASGI apps via router - Modular app composition where teams own routers with their own sub-apps The current behavior silently drops Mount routes with no error and no warning. Your PR (#13944) solves this for StaticFiles and raises a clear error for everything else, which is better than silent failure. Our approach propagates all Mount routes with the correct prefix. The technical difference is one line: `self.mount(prefix + router.prefix + route.path, app=route.app, name=route.name)`. The reason `router.prefix` is needed is that `APIRoute.path` has the prefix baked in at creation time, but `Mount.path` does not. Both approaches are valid. If Sebastian decides StaticFiles-only is the right scope, #13944 is the right PR. If full Mount support is desired, the approach in this PR (and #14793) handles it. Happy to close this if #13944 is the direction the project wants to take.']

review_comments: ['Hi, I implemented a fix for this and added regression tests. The change makes multi-method routes generate distinct OpenAPI `operationId` values per HTTP method, while leaving explicit `operation_id` values untouched. I also verified it with: * `uv run pytest tests/test_generate_unique_id_function.py` * `uv run pytest tests/test_extra_routes.py` I’m opening a PR now. ', ' ## Merging this PR will **not alter performance** `✅ 20` untouched benchmarks --- Comparing abishekgiri:fix/my-first-contribution (1d436a9) with master (3b9d848)[^unexpected-base] [^unexpected-base]: No successful run was found on master (d128a70) during the generation of this report, so 3b9d848 was used instead as the comparison base. There might be some changes unrelated to this pull request in this report. ', 'Thanks! It looks like the failing check is only due to the missing required PR label. This PR is a bug fix, so the appropriate label should be `bug`. I don’t think I have permission to add labels on the upstream repo, so could a maintainer or triager please add the `bug` label? ', 'Please review other PRs linked to the issue and explain why you think your approach is better than others', '> Please review other PRs linked to the issue and explain why you think your approach is better than others I reviewed the other PRs linked to `#13175` (`#13861`, `#14881`, `#15070`, `#15074`, `#15140`, `#15194`, and `#15215`). My reasoning for this approach is: - `#13861`, `#14881`, and `#15070` make the selected method deterministic, but they do not solve the duplicate `operationId` problem itself. - `#15074` appends the current method to `route.unique_id`, which already contains a method suffix in the default generator, so it can produce IDs like `..._post_post`. - `#15140` and `#15194` are closer, but they still have to reverse-engineer a base ID from `route.unique_id`. That is brittle for custom `generate_unique_id_function` values, especially `#15140`, which strips everything after the last underscore. - `#15215` rebuilds the base from `route.name + route.path_format` inside OpenAPI, which bypasses `generate_unique_id_function` for multi-method routes. I chose this implementation because it fixes the root ambiguity instead of patching around it later: - for multi-method routes, the default `unique_id` becomes a shared base - the per-method suffix is added only when emitting OpenAPI - explicit `operation_id` remains unchanged - custom `generate_unique_id_function` is still respected, because OpenAPI starts from `route.unique_id` instead of reconstructing it I think that is cleaner because `route.unique_id` is reused internally beyond `operationId`, so removing the arbitrary “first method” from the canonical route ID is more robust than preserving it and then trying to peel it back off in OpenAPI generation. I also added regression coverage for both the default generator and a custom `generate_unique_id_function`, because that was the main gap in the other proposals. ']

review_comments: ['Hi, I implemented a fix for this and added regression tests. The change makes multi-method routes generate distinct OpenAPI `operationId` values per HTTP method, while leaving explicit `operation_id` values untouched. I also verified it with: * `uv run pytest tests/test_generate_unique_id_function.py` * `uv run pytest tests/test_extra_routes.py` I’m opening a PR now. ', ' ## Merging this PR will **not alter performance** `✅ 20` untouched benchmarks --- Comparing abishekgiri:fix/my-first-contribution (1d436a9) with master (3b9d848)[^unexpected-base] [^unexpected-base]: No successful run was found on master (d128a70) during the generation of this report, so 3b9d848 was used instead as the comparison base. There might be some changes unrelated to this pull request in this report. ', 'Thanks! It looks like the failing check is only due to the missing required PR label. This PR is a bug fix, so the appropriate label should be `bug`. I don’t think I have permission to add labels on the upstream repo, so could a maintainer or triager please add the `bug` label? ', 'Please review other PRs linked to the issue and explain why you think your approach is better than others', '> Please review other PRs linked to the issue and explain why you think your approach is better than others I reviewed the other PRs linked to `#13175` (`#13861`, `#14881`, `#15070`, `#15074`, `#15140`, `#15194`, and `#15215`). My reasoning for this approach is: - `#13861`, `#14881`, and `#15070` make the selected method deterministic, but they do not solve the duplicate `operationId` problem itself. - `#15074` appends the current method to `route.unique_id`, which already contains a method suffix in the default generator, so it can produce IDs like `..._post_post`. - `#15140` and `#15194` are closer, but they still have to reverse-engineer a base ID from `route.unique_id`. That is brittle for custom `generate_unique_id_function` values, especially `#15140`, which strips everything after the last underscore. - `#15215` rebuilds the base from `route.name + route.path_format` inside OpenAPI, which bypasses `generate_unique_id_function` for multi-method routes. I chose this implementation because it fixes the root ambiguity instead of patching around it later: - for multi-method routes, the default `unique_id` becomes a shared base - the per-method suffix is added only when emitting OpenAPI - explicit `operation_id` remains unchanged - custom `generate_unique_id_function` is still respected, because OpenAPI starts from `route.unique_id` instead of reconstructing it I think that is cleaner because `route.unique_id` is reused internally beyond `operationId`, so removing the arbitrary “first method” from the canonical route ID is more robust than preserving it and then trying to peel it back off in OpenAPI generation. I also added regression coverage for both the default generator and a custom `generate_unique_id_function`, because that was the main gap in the other proposals. ']

[{'sha': 'c5bd35c728c32fc7aa28fd650e7673678dd9d3a1', 'message': 'fix: treat empty bearer token as missing credentials in OAuth2 helpers

Authorization: Bearer  (empty token after "Bearer") was returned as ""
instead of triggering the auto_error path. Both OAuth2PasswordBearer and
OAuth2AuthorizationCodeBearer only checked that the scheme was "bearer"
but not that the credential string was non-empty.

RFC 6750 Section 2.1 defines b64token = 1*(...) — at least one character
required. An empty credential is syntactically malformed and should be
treated the same as missing credentials: 401 with auto_error=True, None
with auto_error=False.

Fix: add `not param` to the guard condition in both __call__ methods.

Closes: https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/discussions/15192'}, {'sha': 'ca96fdf2922f0d08bfe468c78024ccedfa6fb937', 'message': "Merge branch 'master' into fix/oauth2-empty-bearer"}]

[{'sha': '7c4b6412438d1059f623a637e8ebc7efc6b29694', 'message': 'Fix duplicate operationId for multi-method routes

When a route is registered with multiple HTTP methods via
add_api_route(..., methods=["POST", "DELETE"]), generate_unique_id()
only uses the first method from the set, causing all methods to share
the same unique_id and producing duplicate operationIds in the OpenAPI
schema along with a UserWarning.

Fix by detecting multi-method routes in get_openapi_operation_metadata
and building a per-method operationId using the current method parameter
(already available in the function), following the same pattern as
generate_unique_id.

Fixes #13175'}, {'sha': 'b2e6f8dc1ec9bc1af69e4b8264829f79cd9eb98a', 'message': 'Add test for unique operationId generation in multi-method routes'}, {'sha': '937fcdffbeef192f8236442ed098dc768ab0696d', 'message': '🎨 Auto format'}]

Please, describe why this approach is better Yes, I reviewed the linked PRs before submitting. **Why this approach differs from rejected PRs:** - **#15070**: Only sorted methods for determinism, didn't make IDs unique per method — not a real fix. - **#15074 / #15140**: Appended method to `route.unique_id`, producing double suffixes like `do_something__put_put`. Our approach rebuilds the base from `route.name + route.path_format` using the same pattern as `generate_unique_id`, so the result is clean: `do_something__put` and `do_something__delete`. - **#10694**: Modifies routing layer. Our fix is more minimal — it stays in `get_openapi_operation_metadata()` where `method` is already available, touching only the OpenAPI generation step. **Known limitation**: The body schema name (`Body_do_something__put`) is still generated at route compilation time and remains non-deterministic for multi-method routes. This would require a separate fix at the routing layer (as #10694 attempts). Our PR addresses only the operationId duplication. Happy to adjust the approach based on your feedback. "]

[{'sha': 'a1a7e1f7b65c59f3e1fea97eb927c9a6b2cdd0a9', 'message': 'fix(routing): thread stream_item_type through include_router()

When a typed SSE or JSONL streaming endpoint is registered via a router,
the OpenAPI schema loses the stream item type because include_router()
re-creates routes via add_api_route() without passing stream_item_type.

This fix threads stream_item_type through add_api_route() and include_router(),
following the strict_content_type pattern.

Fixes #15077'}]

parameter results in a 422 error.

The documentation incorrectly stated that clients could send None as
a query parameter value. In reality, omitting a required parameter
returns 422, and there is no standard HTTP mechanism to pass None
as a query string value.

Changes:
- Updated docs to clarify that None in the type annotation means
  the OpenAPI schema marks the parameter as nullable
- Added info box explaining the actual behavior
- Fixed xfail tests to verify correct 422 behavior instead of
  expecting impossible 200 responses

Closes #12419"}]

[{'sha': '11dc2808067acb71771729205f124570d7205835', 'message': "Fix duplicate operation IDs for routes with multiple methods

When a route is registered with multiple HTTP methods (e.g., methods=['POST', 'DELETE']),
the default generate_unique_id function only uses the first method, causing duplicate
operation IDs in the OpenAPI schema.

This fix ensures that when a route has multiple methods and no explicit operation_id,
each method gets a unique operation ID by appending the current method name.

Fixes #13175"}]

Please, describe why this approach is better', 'Hi @YuriiMotov, yes, I've reviewed the other PRs. Here's a comparison: **Other approaches:** - **#13861, #15070** — Make the method selection deterministic (e.g., sorted), but don't fix the duplicate problem itself. Two methods on the same route still produce the same ID. - **#14827, #15009, #14869** (closed) — Earlier attempts; similar strategies but without robust test coverage. - **#15074** (closed) — Appends the current `method` to `route.unique_id` directly. Since `unique_id` already ends in `_`, this produces IDs like `handler_clear_post_post` for the first method — incorrect. - **#15140** (closed) — Strips everything after the last underscore from `unique_id` as the base. This breaks when route names or paths contain underscores (e.g., `my_handler_/my_path_post` → strips too much). - **#15215** — Rebuilds the base ID from scratch using `re.sub(r"\W", "_", f"{route.name}{route.path_format}")`. This bypasses `generate_unique_id_function` entirely for multi-method routes, ignoring custom ID generators. - **#15222** — Modifies `generate_unique_id` to omit the method suffix when `len(route.methods) > 1`, then appends it per-method in `get_openapi_operation_metadata`. Clean root-level fix, but it changes `route.unique_id` itself, which is used beyond OpenAPI (e.g., client generation, internal routing). This is a more invasive change. **Our approach (#15194):** We fix the issue entirely within `get_openapi_operation_metadata`, which is the correct place: that function is called once per method and already has `method` in scope. We: 1. Detect multi-method routes 2. Strip the first-method suffix from `route.unique_id` to recover the base 3. Append the actual `method` for the current operation The fix is scoped to OpenAPI generation and doesn't change `route.unique_id` or `generate_unique_id`. **Known limitation:** The suffix-stripping logic (`base[: -len(first_method)]`) assumes the default `generate_unique_id_function` format. If a custom `generate_unique_id_function` is used that doesn't end in `_`, the base recovery could be wrong. **#15215 and #15222's approach** of rebuilding the base in `openapi/utils.py` (either from `route.name + route.path_format` or by omitting the suffix in `generate_unique_id`) is arguably cleaner for the custom-generator case. However, #15222's route modifies `unique_id` at a lower level, while #15215 bypasses custom generators. Our approach is the most minimal change: a targeted fix in OpenAPI generation only, with no behavioral change for single-method routes or explicit `operation_id`. If the custom-generator edge case is a concern, we can refine the base-extraction logic to be more robust.']

commits:
[{'sha': '8bfab526a2c015765fb708dd2c63d816a4461edb', 'message': 'fix: reject newline characters in SSE event and id fields

`format_sse_event()` interpolated the `event` and `id` fields into SSE
wire-format bytes without stripping or rejecting newline characters. A
`\n` or `\r\n` in either field injects an additional SSE field-line into
the stream — enabling event-type spoofing and fabricated data payloads
in browser EventSource clients.

The `data` and `comment` fields were already protected via `splitlines()`;
the omission for `event` and `id` was an inconsistency, not a design
decision. The identical bug class was fixed in Hono (GHSA-p6xx-57qc-3wxr).

Changes:
- Add `_check_event_no_newline()` validator and apply it to
  `ServerSentEvent.event` via `AfterValidator`
- Extend `_check_id_no_null()` to also reject `\n` and `\r`
- Strip `\r`/`\n` in `format_sse_event()` for `event` and `id` as
  defense-in-depth (low-level function, bypasses model validators)
- Add 7 regression tests covering LF/CR rejection in both the model
  and the low-level formatting function

Severity: Medium (client-side injection; server unaffected)
CWE: CWE-116 — Improper Encoding for Output in a Different Plaintext Context
OWASP: A03:2021 — Injection
Prior art: Hono GHSA-p6xx-57qc-3wxr (same class, same fix pattern)'}, {'sha': '06eccbf077b76309382371fe9440083bea350d54', 'message': "fix: add null-byte protection to SSE 'event' field for symmetry with 'id'

`_check_event_no_newline` lacked a `\0` check that `_check_id_no_null_or_newline`
has had since the original implementation. Similarly, `format_sse_event()` stripped
`\0` from `id` but not from `event`.

Changes:
- Extend `_check_event_no_newline` to also reject `\0` characters
- Strip `\0` in `format_sse_event()` for `event` (mirrors existing `id` behavior)
- Add `test_server_sent_event_null_event_rejected` regression test

`event` and `id` are now fully symmetric in both the model validator and the
low-level wire formatter."}, {'sha': '9e51a7d6956376104e9ca31a09322083671be9a2', 'message': "fix: raise ValueError in format_sse_event instead of stripping invalid chars

Replace silent .replace() sanitization in format_sse_event() with explicit
ValueError raises for \n, \r, and \0 in the event and id fields.

Previously, format_sse_event() would strip those characters, creating a
behavioral inconsistency with ServerSentEvent's AfterValidator which raises
on the same input. As YuriiMotov pointed out: data allows newlines by SSE
spec design (each line becomes a separate data: frame), but event and id are
single-token fields where a newline is always a protocol violation. Silent
stripping masks bugs at the call site; raising surfaces them where they belong.

Both code paths now fail identically for the same invalid input. Tests updated
from strip-behavior assertions to ValueError assertions, plus two new null-byte
tests covering format_sse_event() directly for event and id."}]
review_comments: ['`_check_id_no_null` now validates both NUL and newline/CR characters, so the function name is misleading. Consider renaming it to reflect all rejected characters (e.g., `_check_id_no_null_or_newline`) to avoid future callers assuming it only checks for `\0`.', '`format_sse_event()` now strips both `\n` and `\r` from the `id` field, but the new tests only assert stripping for `\n`. Add a regression test for `id` containing `\r` (similar to the existing event CR test) so both branches of the new sanitization are covered. ```suggestion def test_format_sse_event_strips_cr_in_id(): from fastapi.sse import format_sse_event result = format_sse_event(id="42\rpwned", data_str="hello") assert b"\rpwned" not in result assert b"id: 42pwned\n" in result ```', '`format_sse_event()` sanitizes `id` for CR/LF now, but it still allows `\0` even though `ServerSentEvent.id` rejects it via `_check_id_no_null`. Since `format_sse_event()` is a low-level API callable without Pydantic validation, consider also rejecting or stripping NULs here to keep behavior consistent and avoid emitting spec-invalid `id:` lines. ```suggestion id = id.replace("\r", "").replace("\n", "").replace("\0", "") ```', 'I find it strange to have these `replace` here. This looks like an alternative to validation logic implemented using `AfterValidator`. @subhashdasyam, could you please clarify your intention?', 'The `replace` calls in `format_sse_event()` are not an alternative to the validator. They cover a different code path. `format_sse_event()` is a public low-level function. The routing layer in `routing.py` calls it directly with pre-validated fields from a `ServerSentEvent`, so in that path the sanitization is a no-op. But `format_sse_event()` can also be called without ever constructing a `ServerSentEvent`, for example from custom integrations or internal tooling that builds SSE frames directly. In that path there is no Pydantic validator in play at all. The intent is two layers with different jobs: the `AfterValidator` is a strict gate at the model boundary, and the `replace` calls in `format_sse_event()` are a safety net for callers who skip the model. This follows the same pattern as the existing `splitlines()` on `data` and `comment`. Those fields have no Pydantic validator, but the function still protects the wire format regardless. The follow-up commit from the comment I received from @SadManFahIm also extended this pattern to cover `\0` for `event`, fixing an asymmetry with `id` that was pointed out in review. Both layers now handle the same character set consistently, which is another sign the two layers serve different purposes rather than duplicating each other.', "@subhashdasyam These aren't duplicating each other — they cover different call paths. The routing layer always goes through a ServerSentEvent, so the AfterValidator fires and the replace calls become a no-op there. But format_sse_event() is a public, low-level function and can be called directly — from custom integrations or internal tooling — without ever constructing a ServerSentEvent. In that path there's no Pydantic validation at all, so the replace calls are the only protection.", '> @subhashdasyam These aren't duplicating each other — they cover different call paths. > > The routing layer always goes through a ServerSentEvent, so the AfterValidator fires and the replace calls become a no-op there. But format_sse_event() is a public, low-level function and can be called directly — from custom integrations or internal tooling — without ever constructing a ServerSentEvent. In that path there's no Pydantic validation at all, so the replace calls are the only protection. On point 1 -- bare `\r` test coverage is already there. `test_server_sent_event_cr_event_rejected` at line 229 covers exactly this case: ```python def test_server_sent_event_cr_event_rejected(): with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="newline"): ServerSentEvent(data="test", event="chat\rpwned") ``` A bare `\r` without `\n`, asserts a `ValueError` is raised. Same pattern exists for `id` in `test_server_sent_event_cr_id_rejected`.', "I don't think `format_sse_event` should be treated as a part of public API as it's not documented and seems to be for internal use only. But yes, not having this guard logic in `format_sse_event` may lead to introducing bugs in the future. Anyway, I believe we should be consistent in how we handle this. In case of `ServerSentEvent` it will raise `ValidationError`, so I think we should follow the same logic for the path without `ServerSentEvent`. What if we just make `format_sse_event` return an instance of `ServerSentEvent`. This way the logic will be consistent and we don't need to duplicate it", "Hey @YuriiMotov , you're right that the inconsistency is a real problem. Let me explain why I didn't go the `ServerSentEvent` route, and what I think actually makes sense here. The trouble with having `format_sse_event` construct a `ServerSentEvent` internally is that the two APIs don't share the same data model. `format_sse_event` takes `data_str` (already serialized data) while `ServerSentEvent` takes `data: Any` and `raw_data: str | None`. There's no clean mapping between them. On top of that, the routing layer calls `format_sse_event` with fields it already pulled from a validated `ServerSentEvent`, so you'd be validating the same thing twice. That seems like the wrong direction. That said, your consistency point stands. If `ServerSentEvent` raises on bad input, `format_sse_event` should too, not silently strip. commits:
[{'sha': '8bfab526a2c015765fb708dd2c63d816a4461edb', 'message': 'fix: reject newline characters in SSE event and id fields

`format_sse_event()` interpolated the `event` and `id` fields into SSE
wire-format bytes without stripping or rejecting newline characters. A
`\n` or `\r\n` in either field injects an additional SSE field-line into
the stream — enabling event-type spoofing and fabricated data payloads
in browser EventSource clients.

The `data` and `comment` fields were already protected via `splitlines()`;
the omission for `event` and `id` was an inconsistency, not a design
decision. The identical bug class was fixed in Hono (GHSA-p6xx-57qc-3wxr).

Changes:
- Add `_check_event_no_newline()` validator and apply it to
  `ServerSentEvent.event` via `AfterValidator`
- Extend `_check_id_no_null()` to also reject `\n` and `\r`
- Strip `\r`/`\n` in `format_sse_event()` for `event` and `id` as
  defense-in-depth (low-level function, bypasses model validators)
- Add 7 regression tests covering LF/CR rejection in both the model
  and the low-level formatting function

Severity: Medium (client-side injection; server unaffected)
CWE: CWE-116 — Improper Encoding for Output in a Different Plaintext Context
OWASP: A03:2021 — Injection
Prior art: Hono GHSA-p6xx-57qc-3wxr (same class, same fix pattern)'}, {'sha': '06eccbf077b76309382371fe9440083bea350d54', 'message': "fix: add null-byte protection to SSE 'event' field for symmetry with 'id'

`_check_event_no_newline` lacked a `\0` check that `_check_id_no_null_or_newline`
has had since the original implementation. Similarly, `format_sse_event()` stripped
`\0` from `id` but not from `event`.

Changes:
- Extend `_check_event_no_newline` to also reject `\0` characters
- Strip `\0` in `format_sse_event()` for `event` (mirrors existing `id` behavior)
- Add `test_server_sent_event_null_event_rejected` regression test

`event` and `id` are now fully symmetric in both the model validator and the
low-level wire formatter."}, {'sha': '9e51a7d6956376104e9ca31a09322083671be9a2', 'message': "fix: raise ValueError in format_sse_event instead of stripping invalid chars

Replace silent .replace() sanitization in format_sse_event() with explicit
ValueError raises for \n, \r, and \0 in the event and id fields.

Previously, format_sse_event() would strip those characters, creating a
behavioral inconsistency with ServerSentEvent's AfterValidator which raises
on the same input. As YuriiMotov pointed out: data allows newlines by SSE
spec design (each line becomes a separate data: frame), but event and id are
single-token fields where a newline is always a protocol violation. Silent
stripping masks bugs at the call site; raising surfaces them where they belong.

Both code paths now fail identically for the same invalid input. Tests updated
from strip-behavior assertions to ValueError assertions, plus two new null-byte
tests covering format_sse_event() directly for event and id."}]
review_comments: ['`_check_id_no_null` now validates both NUL and newline/CR characters, so the function name is misleading. Consider renaming it to reflect all rejected characters (e.g., `_check_id_no_null_or_newline`) to avoid future callers assuming it only checks for `\0`.', '`format_sse_event()` now strips both `\n` and `\r` from the `id` field, but the new tests only assert stripping for `\n`. Add a regression test for `id` containing `\r` (similar to the existing event CR test) so both branches of the new sanitization are covered. ```suggestion def test_format_sse_event_strips_cr_in_id(): from fastapi.sse import format_sse_event result = format_sse_event(id="42\rpwned", data_str="hello") assert b"\rpwned" not in result assert b"id: 42pwned\n" in result ```', '`format_sse_event()` sanitizes `id` for CR/LF now, but it still allows `\0` even though `ServerSentEvent.id` rejects it via `_check_id_no_null`. Since `format_sse_event()` is a low-level API callable without Pydantic validation, consider also rejecting or stripping NULs here to keep behavior consistent and avoid emitting spec-invalid `id:` lines. ```suggestion id = id.replace("\r", "").replace("\n", "").replace("\0", "") ```', 'I find it strange to have these `replace` here. This looks like an alternative to validation logic implemented using `AfterValidator`. @subhashdasyam, could you please clarify your intention?', 'The `replace` calls in `format_sse_event()` are not an alternative to the validator. They cover a different code path. `format_sse_event()` is a public low-level function. The routing layer in `routing.py` calls it directly with pre-validated fields from a `ServerSentEvent`, so in that path the sanitization is a no-op. But `format_sse_event()` can also be called without ever constructing a `ServerSentEvent`, for example from custom integrations or internal tooling that builds SSE frames directly. In that path there is no Pydantic validator in play at all. The intent is two layers with different jobs: the `AfterValidator` is a strict gate at the model boundary, and the `replace` calls in `format_sse_event()` are a safety net for callers who skip the model. This follows the same pattern as the existing `splitlines()` on `data` and `comment`. Those fields have no Pydantic validator, but the function still protects the wire format regardless. The follow-up commit from the comment I received from @SadManFahIm also extended this pattern to cover `\0` for `event`, fixing an asymmetry with `id` that was pointed out in review. Both layers now handle the same character set consistently, which is another sign the two layers serve different purposes rather than duplicating each other.', "@subhashdasyam These aren't duplicating each other — they cover different call paths. The routing layer always goes through a ServerSentEvent, so the AfterValidator fires and the replace calls become a no-op there. But format_sse_event() is a public, low-level function and can be called directly — from custom integrations or internal tooling — without ever constructing a ServerSentEvent. In that path there's no Pydantic validation at all, so the replace calls are the only protection.", '> @subhashdasyam These aren't duplicating each other — they cover different call paths. > > The routing layer always goes through a ServerSentEvent, so the AfterValidator fires and the replace calls become a no-op there. On point 1 -- bare `\r` test coverage is already there. `test_server_sent_event_cr_event_rejected` at line 229 covers exactly this case: ```python def test_server_sent_event_cr_event_rejected(): with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="newline"): ServerSentEvent(data="test", event="chat\rpwned") ``` A bare `\r` without `\n`, asserts a `ValueError` is raised. Same pattern exists for `id` in `test_server_sent_event_cr_id_rejected`.', "I don't think `format_sse_event` should be treated as a part of public API as it's not documented and seems to be for internal use only. But yes, not having this guard logic in `format_sse_event` may lead to introducing bugs in the future. Anyway, I believe we should be consistent in how we handle this. Let me explain why I didn't go the `ServerSentEvent` route, and what I think actually makes sense here. The trouble with having `format_sse_event` construct a `ServerSentEvent` internally is that the two APIs don't share the same data model. `format_sse_event` takes `data_str` (already serialized data) while `ServerSentEvent` takes `data: Any` and `raw_data: str | None`. There's no clean mapping between them. On top of that, the routing layer calls `format_sse_event` with fields it already pulled from a validated `ServerSentEvent`, so you'd be validating the same thing twice. That seems like the wrong direction. That said, your consistency point stands. If `ServerSentEvent` raises on bad input, `format_sse_event` should too, not silently strip. [{'sha': '3e93e805f630ddcb46f79be00c97dbe28d22b6d4', 'message': 'fix: resolve ForwardRef in get_typed_annotation for Annotated+Depends (#13056)

When using from __future__ import annotations with Annotated[Potato, Depends(...)],
the annotation can reach analyze_param as a ForwardRef. Previously only str was
resolved; ForwardRef was returned as-is, causing Depends to be missed and the
param to be treated as a Query param. This led to PydanticUserError during
OpenAPI schema generation.

Extend get_typed_annotation to also resolve ForwardRef before returning.'}]

When using from __future__ import annotations with Annotated[Potato, Depends(...)],
the annotation can reach analyze_param as a ForwardRef. Previously only str was
resolved; ForwardRef was returned as-is, causing Depends to be missed and the
param to be treated as a Query param. This led to PydanticUserError during
OpenAPI schema generation.

Extend get_typed_annotation to also resolve ForwardRef before returning.'}]

review:
[
  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "在 `test_openapi_schema` 测试中，使用 `IsOneOf` 来断言 OpenAPI schema 的 `components.schemas` 部分，但 `IsOneOf` 的第一个分支（对应 Pydantic >= 2.11）的描述为 'no top-level OtherRole'，然而其 `User.role` 字段的 `anyOf` 数组中仍然包含了一个 `{\"enum\": [], \"title\": \"OtherRole\"}` 的引用。这可能导致测试断言失败，因为如果 Pydantic >= 2.11 确实不再生成顶层的 `OtherRole` schema，那么这个内联的 `enum` 对象可能无法正确匹配。测试逻辑存在不一致性。",
    "evidence": "在 `test_openapi_schema` 函数中，`components.schemas` 被断言为 `IsOneOf`，其第一个分支（Pydantic >= 2.11）的 `User.role.anyOf` 数组包含 `{\"enum\": [], \"title\": \"OtherRole\"}`，但该分支的顶层 schemas 对象中并没有 `OtherRole` 键。",
    "advice": "审查 Pydantic >= 2.11 版本的实际行为，确认 `enum` 字段为空的 `OtherRole` 是否应该出现在 `anyOf` 数组中，以及它是否需要一个对应的顶层 schema 定义。根据 Pydantic 的实际输出调整 `IsOneOf` 的预期值，确保测试能准确反映不同 Pydantic 版本的行为差异。",
    "confidence": 0.85,
    "severity": "Medium"
  },
  {
    "type": "Readability",
    "description": "在 `test_union_body_discriminator` 测试中，对 `FirstItem.value` 和 `OtherItem.value` 属性的 schema 使用了 `IsOneOf` 断言，但两个分支的注释存在不一致。对于 `FirstItem.value`，注释将 `const only` 分支关联到 'Pydantic >= 2.10'，将 `const + enum` 分支关联到 'Pydantic 2.9'。然而，对于 `OtherItem.value`，注释将 `const only` 分支关联到 'Pydantic >= 2.10.0'，将 `const + enum` 分支关联到 'Pydantic 2.9.x'。这种微小的不一致（'2.10' vs '2.10.0', '2.9' vs '2.9.x'）虽然不影响功能，但降低了代码的可读性和维护性，可能使其他开发者困惑。",
    "evidence": "在 `test_union_body_discriminator` 函数中，`FirstItem.value` 的注释为 '# Pydantic >= 2.10: const only' 和 '# Pydantic 2.9: const + enum'，而 `OtherItem.value` 的注释为 '# Pydantic >= 2.10.0: const only' 和 '# Pydantic 2.9.x: const + enum'。",
    "advice": "统一注释的格式，使其在版本号表述上保持一致。例如，可以统一使用 'Pydantic >= 2.10' 和 'Pydantic 2.9'，或者都加上 '.x' 后缀。这有助于提高代码的清晰度。",
    "confidence": 0.9,
    "severity": "Low"
  },
  {
    "type": "Design",
    "description": "代码变更在测试中引入了 `dirty_equals.IsOneOf` 来灵活匹配不同 Pydantic 版本生成的 OpenAPI schema 差异。这符合 FastAPI 项目支持多个 Pydantic 版本并保持测试健壮性的设计目标。然而，需要确保这些 `IsOneOf` 断言覆盖了所有受支持的 Pydantic 版本范围，并且每个分支的预期值都准确无误。当前变更看起来是为了修复 CI 中因 Pydantic 版本差异导致的测试失败，这是合理的。",
    "evidence": "在两个测试文件 (`test_schema_compat_pydantic_v2.py` 和 `test_union_body_discriminator.py`) 的变更中，都引入了 `from dirty_equals import IsOneOf` 并使用它来包装 schema 断言，以处理 Pydantic 2.9, 2.10, 2.11 等版本间的行为变化。",
    "advice": "确保测试覆盖了项目所支持的所有 Pydantic 版本（例如，通过检查 `pyproject.toml` 或 CI 配置中的版本约束）。考虑添加注释说明这些差异的具体 Pydantic 版本号变更点，以方便未来维护。",
    "confidence": 0.8,
    "severity": "Low"
  }
]

    "description": "新增的 `core_schema` 属性在尝试解析 `ForwardRef` 时，通过遍历 `sys.modules` 并合并所有模块的 `__dict__` 来构建命名空间。这种方法存在严重问题：1. 它合并了所有已加载模块的全局命名空间，这可能导致命名冲突和意外行为，因为不同模块中可能存在同名的类或函数。2. 它没有考虑模块的导入顺序或作用域，可能导致解析到错误的类型。3. 当 `sys.modules` 中包含 `None` 值或某些模块的 `__dict__` 不可访问时，会捕获并忽略所有 `Exception`，这可能会掩盖重要的错误。4. 这种方法性能开销大，每次访问 `core_schema` 属性时，如果遇到未解析的 `ForwardRef`，都会遍历所有模块。这可能导致在生成 OpenAPI 模式时出现不可预测的行为和潜在的错误。",

commits:
[{'sha': '2ea91122c9960de437498a5c7ac83cca4a064cfc', 'message': 'Emit UserWarning when Response.background silently discards injected BackgroundTasks

When an endpoint injects BackgroundTasks via dependency injection AND
returns a Response that already has its own `background` attribute set,
the injected tasks are silently dropped. This is a confusing footgun
that causes tasks to disappear without any indication.

This change adds a UserWarning in that scenario so the silent data loss
becomes visible. The warning message explains how to resolve the
conflict (either use the injected BackgroundTasks exclusively, or stop
injecting BackgroundTasks when using Response.background directly).

Ref: https://github.com/fastapi/fastapi/issues/11215

Co-Authored-By: Claude (claude-opus-4-6) <noreply@anthropic.com>'}, {'sha': 'cb0c5f89afdc062531bf68ab471c141d2ac57f0d', 'message': '🎨 Auto format'}]
